data.title: DocuSign 'dilemma' comes to light as Wall Street frets over future
data.description:
DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU) was in focus on Friday after the electronic signature company lowered its full-year billings guidance, causing Wall Street analysts to express some concern about its near-term future.

Shares plunged 18.5% in premarket trading.

“DOCU's dilemma is the [near-term] disconnect between results/KPIs with [management's] tone & [intelligent agreement management] bullishness,” Morgan Stanley analyst Josh Baer wrote in a note to clients. “It may be timing, in which case patient investors will be rewarded.”

Baer, who has an Equal-Weight rating on DocuSign, lowered his price target to $86 from $92, said more evidence is needed that the bullishness for intelligent agreement management is real.

“For the neutral, less-patient investor who is waiting for more evidence IAM can clearly be incremental to the model, staying on the sidelines for the next quarter makes sense, unlikely to miss a material move higher until 2HFY26 at the earliest,” Baer wrote.

He continued: “In our view, we side closest with the neutral investor – and given some of the bear case concerns, we are waiting for more evidence (to show up in our primary checks/diligence, results/KPIs) before underwriting the bullish IAM-driven growth acceleration to double digits – and therefore, we remain [Equal Weight].”

Evercore ISI analyst Kirk Materne said DocuSign is “moving in the right direction,” but the billings weakness is an issue investors will have to contend with for a while.

“While the billings miss and guide down is weighing on the stock this morning, management seems comfortable with the overall trends in the business and views the billings shortfall as more of an unforced error on their part — in terms of not de-risking for less early renewals in F1Q — vs. a fundamental shift in the business,” Materne wrote.

“In general, we believe the reaction today is perhaps a little overdone if taking a longer-term view, but until the Street builds greater confidence in the adjusted outlook, shares could remain in a bit of a trading range.”

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Broadcom, Alphabet, T-Mobile US, Travelers, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Consolidated Water (CWCO) and Essential Properties (ERPT) as well as declarations from companies such as Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) and T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Travelers (NYSE:TRV) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Consolidated Water (CWCO)

  • Increase: 27.3%

Essential Properties (EPRT)

  • Dividend declared: June 2

Autoliv (ALV)

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Vertiv (VRT)

T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS)

Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO)

  • Dividend declared: June 5

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL)

Travelers (NYSE:TRV)

Kohl's (KSS)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 11
  • Payout date: June 25

Pimco (PCN)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 12
  • Payout date: July 1

data.title: Global gold ETF flows turn negative in May, total outflows hit $1.8 billion
data.description:
Global physically backed gold ETFs lost $1.8 billion in May, snapping their five-month inflow streak.

North America led the decline with $1.5 billion in outflows, followed by Asia with $489 million, while Europe bucked the trend, recording $225 million in inflows, the World Gold Council said. 

The first monthly outflow since last November and a mild fall in the gold price also saw global gold ETFs’ total assets under management (AUM) fall 1% to $374 billion. 

The better-than-expected temporary easing of tariffs between the US and China improved investor risk appetite, which led to a strong rebound in equities, but lower safe-haven demand for gold.

Gold prices ended the month of May almost flat (-0.7%) at $3,278 an ounce, albeit with some intra-month volatility. Year-to-date, gold remains up a staggering 26%.

The US Fed kept rates steady at its May meeting; minutes showed a cautious stance towards rate decisions amid an outlook of persistent inflation and risks to the labour market.

As a result, the market is now expecting higher rates by the end of 2025, leading to rising US Treasury yields and increasing the opportunity cost of holding gold. 

Gold market trading volumes averaged $363 billion/day in May, 18% lower m/m yet significantly higher than the 2024 average of $233 billion/day.

Total net longs of COMEX gold futures meanwhile reached 551t by the end of May, a mild 3% decline m/m. 

More Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), (DUST)

data.title: What analysts are saying about Tesla after the Elon Musk-President Trump spectacle
data.description:
Analysts are digging into the ramifications of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk's explosive feud with President Trump on Thursday. While the barrage of insults and accusations has stopped, the question remains for investors if the drama will impact the stock?

Citi Index analyst Fiona Cincotta said it was obvious that threats lobbed back and forth between Musk and Trump are unlikely to come into fruition. "I don’t expect this to blow out into anything more serious than a war of words for a couple of days," highlighted Cincotta.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said the feud does not change the firm's bullish view of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and the autonomous view, but did see it as a fly in the ointment of the Trump regulatory framework going forward.

Taking a more cautious view, CFRA analyst Garrett Nelson said the selloff reflects a number of other factors like an unjustified run-up in Tesla's (TSLA) following its Q1 earnings release, ongoing market share losses in China and Europe, and a realization that next week’s Robotaxi launch in Austin could disappoint.

Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs lowered its price target on Tesla (TSLA) to $285 from $295, due mainly to weaker monthly data from the U.S., Europe, and China.

On Seeking Alpha, analyst KM Capital is positive on Tesla's (TSLA) upcoming robotaxi launch. "Even if Tesla struggles to beat Waymo and remains number two in the industry, it will still create substantial value for shareholders as the industry is expected to demonstrate explosive growth," noted KM Capital.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were up 4.1% in premarket trading after shedding 14.3% on Thursday.

data.title: Rise-hailing service Marti Technologies rises 6%, touches 2.15M riders
data.description:
• Marti Technologies (NYSE:MRT) ride-hailing service has reached 2.15M riders and 314K registered drivers.
  • The company said that it targets 2.15M riders and 310K registered drivers.
  • Marti’s number of ride-hailing riders grew 12.7% from March 25, 2025, to June 3, 2025. 
  • The company now targets 2.50M riders and 350K registered drivers by September 30, 2025.
  • The stock price traded 6.1% on Friday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: ABM Industries Non-GAAP EPS of $0.86 in-line, revenue of $2.1B beats by $40M
data.description:
• ABM Industries press release (NYSE:ABM): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.86 in-line.
  • Revenue of $2.1B (+4.6% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • The Company is reaffirming its outlook for fiscal year 2025 adjusted EPS to be in the range of $3.65 to $3.80. The projected full year adjusted EBITDA margin also remains unchanged at 6.3% to 6.5%. This outlook does not give effect to any potential positive or negative prior year self-insurance adjustments.

data.title: Indian central bank front-loads rate cut to boost growth amid global volatility
data.description:
Indian stocks rallied on Friday after the central bank delivered a bigger-than-expected interest rate cut, as it front-loaded policy easing to support domestic growth in the "fragile and highly fluid" global backdrop.  

The Reserve Bank of India lowered its policy repo rate by 50 basis points to 5.5%, the lowest level since 2022 and its third consecutive cut. Following the decision, the benchmark Nifty 50 (NIFTY) logged its best trading session in three weeks to end 1% higher. BSE Sensex (SENSEX) closed up 0.9%.

At a press conference, RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said "front-loading" rate cuts was needed to support economic growth, given heightened global uncertainty.

"Strong macroeconomic fundamentals and benign inflation outlook provide space for monetary policy to support growth, while remaining consistent with the goal of price stability," he said. "Today's policy action should be seen as a step towards propelling growth to a higher aspirational trajectory."

Malhotra added that after cutting rates by 100 bps since February 2025, the RBI has changed its stance from accommodative to neutral. It will assess incoming data and the evolving outlook to chart out future policy.

"The RBI is signalling a pause, but it's leaving the door open for more easing if growth or inflation weakens further," said Deepali Bhargava, chief economist - Asia-Pacific at ING. "… with real policy rates still sitting well above historical norms, we continue to expect one 25 bp rate cut later this year, likely in Q4."

The RBI's policy decision echoes recent moves by central banks in China, South Korea and Indonesia to stimulate domestic growth amid global uncertainty.

data.title: Vera plunges as Otsuka outperforms with IgA nephropathy therapy
data.description:
Vera Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VERA) fell ~32% in the premarket on Friday after late-stage trial data indicated that the company’s lead asset, atacicept, targeted at a kidney disorder called immunoglobulin A nephropathy failed to outperform a rival product from Otsuka Pharmaceutical (OTCPK:OTSKY).

The selloff came after Otsuka, a unit of Otsuka Holdings (OTCPK:OTSKY), presented interim data from a late-stage trial involving nearly 510 adults with IgA nephropathy at a European medical event to support its rival therapy, sibeprenlimab.

Citing nine-month data in its Phase 3 VISIONARY study, Otsuka said that sibeprenlimab led to a 51.2% (P<0.0001) decline in proteinuria, an endpoint used in IgAN clinical trials to support accelerated regulatory approvals.

The effect measured using 24-hour uPCR (urine protein-to-creatinine ratio) and related to the study's main goal was statistically significant and clinically meaningful, the company added.

While ~76% of patients on sibeprenlimab experienced treatment-emergent adverse events compared to ~85% in the placebo arm, the experimental treatment indicated a safety profile consistent with prior data, Otsuka (OTCPK:OTSKY) noted.

The company’s Biologics License Application targeting U.S. marketing approval of sibeprenlimab for IgA nephropathy is currently under FDA priority review with a target action date of Nov. 28, 2025.

On Monday, Vera (NASDAQ:VERA) announced that its IgAN therapy met the primary endpoint in its ORIGIN Phase 3 trial, demonstrating a statistically significant 42% reduction in proteinuria compared to placebo at week 36.

data.title: Wynn Resorts enters the UK gaming market with a casino in London
data.description:
Wynn Resorts Limited (NASDAQ:WYNN) announced that it completed the purchase of Wynn Mayfair from Crown Resorts after landing the required approval from the UK Gaming Commission. Notably, the prestigious Mayfair casino location expands Wynn's (NASDAQ:WYNN) footprint in Europe and the Middle East ahead of Wynn Al Marjan Island resort opening in the UAE in early 2027.

Wynn Mayfair spans two historic townhouses at 27-28 Curzon Street in London. The location is considered one of the most exclusive gaming and dining experiences in the region. For more than half a century, London's brightest stars, royals, and the society set have flocked to the private members-only club.

The club presents various gaming options with twenty gaming tables distributed across the main gaming floor, rooftop terrace, and six opulent salons privé. The club also contains an impressive collection of antiques, plush furnishings, and both classical and contemporary works by photographers and artists. 

The casino company thinks the acquisition of the iconic asset will give it a presence in a global gateway city, as well as creating a conduit for guests to visit other resorts, particularly Wynn Al Marjan Island which is slated to open in the first quarter of 2027 in Ras Al Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

CBRE Equity Research analyst John DeCree highlighted that the deal could be a valuable customer acquisition tool for Wynn Resorts (WYNN). He noted that Aspinalls probably does not generate much cash flow, but sees the casino pickup as a strategic purchase. "Aspinalls caters to very high-end VIP gaming customers from London but also internationally, including from Asia, India, and the Middle East, making it a very unique customer acquisition tool for Wynn Al Marjan Island," he stated. DeCree highlighted that Aspinalls hosts a regular Bollywood Night, and India is one of the top feeder markets for tourism to the UAE.

"This acquisition gives WYNN a flag in a gateway city that should help build the global database and boost Wynn's brand power in the region ahead of a blockbuster opening in the UAE. Importantly, this is a high-end gaming asset with high-intent customers, making it a more deliberate customer acquisition channel relative to a branded hotel strategy or partnership."

Shares of Wynn Resorts (WYNN) traded flat at $83.25 in the premarket session.
